activities.name: Check in — Kyoto Hotel
activities.notes: Drop bags, freshen up.

activities.name: Nishiki Market
activities.notes: Kyoto's 'Kitchen' — narrow covered market with tofu, pickles, street snacks, and local ingredients. 5 blocks long.

activities.name: Weekenders Coffee
activities.notes: One of Kyoto's best specialty coffee spots. Small, cozy, excellent pour-overs.

activities.name: Pontocho Alley Stroll
activities.notes: Narrow lantern-lit alley along the Kamo River. One of Kyoto's most atmospheric streets.

activities.name: 🍴 Honke Daiichi-Asahi
activities.notes: Legendary Kyoto ramen — shoyu broth, thin noodles, char siu pork. Often a queue but moves fast. Near Kyoto Station.
activities.place: Honke Daiichi Asahi, Kyoto Station, Kyoto, Japan

activities.name: 🍴 Chao Chao Gyoza
activities.notes: Late-night gyoza spot near Gion. Crispy pan-fried dumplings, cold beer.
activities.place: Chao Chao Gyoza, Gion, Kyoto, Japan

activities.name: Travel to Arashiyama
activities.notes: Walk to Sanjo Keihan Station → Subway Tozai Line West to Uzumasa Tenjingawa → Randen Tram to Arashiyama. (~35 mins)

activities.name: Arashiyama Monkey Park Iwatayama
activities.notes: Hike up (20 mins), see monkeys, view the city. Walk 5 mins from station to entrance.
activities.place: Iwatayama Monkey Park, Arashiyama, Kyoto, Japan

activities.name: 🍴 Arashiyama Yoshimura
activities.notes: Soba with river view, or street food near the bridge.

activities.name: Taxi to Otagi Nenbutsu-ji
activities.notes: Taxi approx ¥1,500 from Arashiyama main street. Walking takes 45 mins uphill — taxi is better.

activities.name: Otagi Nenbutsuji Temple
activities.notes: Hidden gem with 1,200 statues. Quiet and atmospheric.
activities.place: Otagi Nenbutsu-ji, Sagadaikakujimonzen Toriimoto, Ukyo Ward, Kyoto, Japan

activities.name: Saga-Toriimoto
activities.notes: Walk downhill from Otagi back through the Saga-Toriimoto preserved street. Beautiful and far less crowded than the Bamboo Grove.

activities.name: Return to Hotel
activities.notes: JR Saga-Arashiyama or Randen back to Sanjo.

activities.name: 🍴 Dinner
activities.notes: 5-min walk from hotel. Narrow alley — Yakiniku, Izakaya, Kyoto cuisine.

activities.name: ⛩ Fushimi Inari Taisha
activities.notes: Start as early as possible — arriving by 6 AM is highly recommended to hike the thousands of vermilion torii gates before the tour buses arrive.

activities.name: Tofuku-ji Temple
activities.notes: Renowned for its spectacular zen gardens and wooden bridge.

activities.name: Kiyomizu-dera
activities.notes: Views from the massive wooden stage. Walk downhill through the beautifully preserved Sannenzaka and Ninenzaka streets.

activities.name: Gion District
activities.notes: Stroll down Hanamikoji Street at dusk — best chance to spot a geisha or maiko heading to evening appointments.

activities.name: 🎍 Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
activities.notes: 7 AM gives a magical, empty window. Right next door: Tenryu-ji Temple stunning gardens.

activities.name: Katsura River & Togetsukyo Bridge
activities.notes: Cross the scenic Togetsukyo Bridge. Grab a coffee at % ARABICA Kyoto Arashiyama and enjoy the waterfront views.

activities.name: Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion)
activities.notes: Pair with Ryoan-ji next door — quieter, highly meditative rock garden.

activities.name: 🍴 Honke Daiichi-Asahi
activities.notes: Legendary Kyoto ramen for dinner.
activities.place: Honke Daiichi Asahi, Kyoto Station, Kyoto, Japan

activities.name: Nara Day Stop
activities.notes: JR Nara Line from Kyoto (~45 min). Todai-ji (giant Buddha), deer in Nara Park, Kasuga Taisha Shrine.

activities.name: Uji Day Stop
activities.notes: JR Nara Line from Nara to Uji (~30 min). Byodoin Temple (10-yen coin), Uji Bridge, matcha ice cream.

activities.name: Travel to Osaka
activities.notes: JR from Uji to Osaka Namba (~1h). Check in to Hotel Forza Osaka Namba.
